message:
feat(writing/slack-mattermost): land the article body, page, route, and content registration

Completes the slack-mattermost-migration article that was started in
the previous commit (jargon dictionary, visualizations, OG/Twitter
share images). Now buildable end-to-end.

components/slack-migration-article.tsx (NEW, 705 LOC, "use client")
  - The article body. Loads three Google fonts (Crimson Pro for serif
    body, JetBrains Mono for code, Bricolage Grotesque for headings),
    pulls jargon entries via getJargon (hover-tooltip popovers backed
    by lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts), composes lucide icons
    (CheckCircle2, Hash, Lock, MessagesSquare, Rocket, Sparkles)
    against the article's narrative beats, and dynamically imports
    the four visualization components from
    ./slack-migration-visualizations with `{ ssr: false }` because
    they touch browser-only APIs (IntersectionObserver, Element
    measurements) and framer-motion's layout effects.
  - Implements its own scroll-progress reading via the shared
    getScrollMetrics() helper so the in-article reading bar stays
    in sync with the rest of the site.

app/writing/slack-mattermost-migration/page.tsx
  - The route component composes JsonLd (Article schema with
    datePublished 2026-04-16) + the SlackMigrationArticle body.
    Was held back from the previous commit because the article
    component didn't exist yet — now they ship together.

app/writing/[slug]/page.tsx
  - Registers "slack-mattermost-migration" in
    STATIC_WRITING_ROUTE_SLUGS so the dynamic [slug] route knows
    not to attempt to render this slug through the generic writing
    handler. Also adds "overprompting" and "raptorq" while we're
    here — those slugs were already live but missing from the
    static set, so the dynamic handler was racing the static
    routes and occasionally returning the generic content shell
    on first hit.

lib/content.ts
  - New entry at the head of writingHighlights so the article shows
    up first on the writing index page. Uses the
    purple→cyan→emerald accent gradient that matches the
    article's own color system (Slack purple → Mattermost cyan →
    "ready / saved" emerald).
  - featured: true, category: "AI Agents & Infrastructure",
    blurb under 200 chars covering the three load-bearing claims
    (paired skills, fail-closed gate, ~99% lower ongoing cost).

app/globals.css (+373 LOC)
  - Adds the .sm-scope CSS namespace that the article locks all
    its custom properties under (--sm-bg, --sm-text, --sm-muted,
    plus the gradient stops). Scoping to .sm-scope prevents the
    article's heavy color system from leaking into the rest of
    the site — this is the same scoping pattern the bakery /
    barra / cmaes articles use.

components/slack-migration-visualizations.tsx
  - Drops the now-unused `useRef` import. Pure cleanup driven by
    the article body landing — the viz components never ended up
    needing a ref handle now that scroll metrics live in the
    article-side wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+import type { Metadata } from "next";
+import { JsonLd } from "@/components/json-ld";
+import { SlackMigrationArticle } from "@/components/slack-migration-article";
+
+export const metadata: Metadata = {
+  title: "Using AI Agents and Skills to Migrate Off Slack | Jeffrey Emanuel",
+  description:
+    "How two paired Claude Code / Codex skills move a company from Slack to a self-hosted Mattermost — end to end, driven by an agent, with a fail-closed readiness gate, a named rollback owner, and 96-99% lower ongoing cost.",
+  alternates: {
+    canonical: "/writing/slack-mattermost-migration",
+  },
+};
+
+const articleSchema = {
+  "@context": "https://schema.org",
+  "@type": "Article",
+  headline: "Using AI Agents and Skills to Migrate Off Slack",
+  datePublished: "2026-04-16",
+  dateModified: "2026-04-16",
+  description:
+    "How two paired Claude Code / Codex skills move a company from Slack to a self-hosted Mattermost — end to end, driven by an agent, with a fail-closed readiness gate, a named rollback owner, and 96-99% lower ongoing cost.",
+  author: {
+    "@type": "Person",
+    name: "Jeffrey Emanuel",
+    url: "https://jeffreyemanuel.com",
+  },
+  publisher: {
+    "@type": "Organization",
+    name: "Jeffrey Emanuel",
+    logo: {
+      "@type": "ImageObject",
+      url: "https://jeffreyemanuel.com/icon-192.png",
+    },
+  },
+  mainEntityOfPage: {
+    "@type": "WebPage",
+    "@id": "https://jeffreyemanuel.com/writing/slack-mattermost-migration",
+  },
+  about: [
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "Slack Migration" },
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "Mattermost" },
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "AI Agents" },
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "Agent Skills" },
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "Self-Hosted Infrastructure" },
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "Claude Code" },
+    { "@type": "Thing", name: "Codex" },
+  ],
+};
+
+export default function SlackMigrationPage() {
+  return (
+    <>
+      <JsonLd data={articleSchema} />
+      <SlackMigrationArticle />
+    </>
+  );
+}
